title: Contributing to heteroskedasticity_identification
text: Thank interest contributing heteroskedasticity_identification R package! document provides guidelines workflows contributing.

title: Contributing to heteroskedasticity_identification
text: follow GitHub Flow branching strategy, simple effective R package development.

title: Contributing to heteroskedasticity_identification
text: main - stable, production-ready branch. releases made branch. Feature branches - Short-lived branches developing new features fixes.

title: Contributing to heteroskedasticity_identification
text: Use descriptive branch names prefixes: feature/ - New features (e.g., feature/add-robust-variance) fix/ - Bug fixes (e.g., fix/issue-42) docs/ - Documentation updates (e.g., docs/improve-vignette) test/ - Test additions improvements (e.g., test/coverage-improvement)

title: Contributing to heteroskedasticity_identification
code: # Fork the repository on GitHub, then: git clone https://github.com/YOUR-USERNAME/heteroskedasticity_identification.git cd heteroskedasticity_identification git remote add upstream https://github.com/fernando-duarte/heteroskedasticity_identification.git

title: Contributing to heteroskedasticity_identification
code: # Ensure your main branch is up to date git checkout main git pull upstream main  # Create a new branch for your feature git checkout -b feature/your-feature-name

title: Contributing to heteroskedasticity_identification
text: Write clear, documented code following tidyverse style guide Add update tests new functionality Update documentation (roxygen2 comments, vignettes, NEWS.md) Ensure code passes checks

title: Contributing to heteroskedasticity_identification
code: # Run checks locally before pushing devtools::check() devtools::test() devtools::document()

title: Contributing to heteroskedasticity_identification
text: project uses pre-commit hooks maintain code quality. cloning: hooks automatically format code check common issues commit.
code: # Install pre-commit (requires Python) pip install pre-commit  # Install the git hooks pre-commit install  # (Optional) Run hooks on all files to check pre-commit run --all-files

title: Contributing to heteroskedasticity_identification
code: # Stage and commit with a clear message git add . git commit -m "Add feature: brief description of changes"

title: Contributing to heteroskedasticity_identification
text: create pull request GitHub branch main.
code: # Push to your fork git push origin feature/your-feature-name

title: Contributing to heteroskedasticity_identification
text: Use clear, descriptive title: - ✅ “Add function White’s heteroskedasticity test” - ❌ “Update code”

title: Contributing to heteroskedasticity_identification
text: Include: - Summary changes - Related issue numbers (fixes #123) - Testing performed - breaking changes

title: Contributing to heteroskedasticity_identification
text: submitting, ensure: - [ ] Pre-commit hooks pass (pre-commit run ---files) - [ ] Code follows tidyverse style guide (enforced styler) - [ ] tests pass (devtools::test()) - [ ] R CMD check passes (devtools::check()) - [ ] Documentation updated - [ ] NEWS.md updated (user-facing changes) - [ ] Examples work correctly

title: Contributing to heteroskedasticity_identification
text: open PR, GitHub Actions automatically: - Run R CMD check multiple platforms (Windows, macOS, Linux) - Run tests coverage reporting - Check code coverage - Build documentation checks must pass merging.

title: Contributing to heteroskedasticity_identification
text: follow tidyverse style guide. Key points: Use <- assignment (=) Use spaces around operators Use descriptive variable names Document exported functions roxygen2

title: Contributing to heteroskedasticity_identification
text: Write tests new functionality Use testthat unit tests Aim high test coverage Test edge cases error conditions

title: Contributing to heteroskedasticity_identification
text: Use roxygen2 function documentation Include examples function documentation Update vignettes major features Keep README.md current

title: Contributing to heteroskedasticity_identification
text: Releases managed maintainers: Features developed feature branches PRs merged main review Version bumped DESCRIPTION NEWS.md updated GitHub release created tag pkgdown site automatically updated

title: Contributing to heteroskedasticity_identification
text: need help: - Check existing issues discussions - Open new issue minimal reproducible example - Ask questions discussions

title: Contributing to heteroskedasticity_identification
text: Please note project released Contributor Code Conduct. participating project agree abide terms. Thank contributing! 🎉

title: Authors and Citation
text: author1, author2, author3 (publicationYear) articleTitle, *journalName*; volumeNumber, [linkToPublication](linkToPublication)
code: @Article{,   entry = {Article},   title = {articleTitle},   author = {{author1} and {author2} and {author3}},   journal = {journalName},   year = {publicationYear},   volume = {volume},   number = {number},   pages = {pages},   url = {linkToPublication}, }

title: A package for identification through heteroskedasticity a la Lewbel (2012)
text: hetid package implements identification heteroskedasticity method Lewbel (2012) time-series models endogenous regressors. provides tools estimation inference traditional instruments available.

title: A package for identification through heteroskedasticity a la Lewbel (2012)
text: can install development version hetid GitHub :

title: A package for identification through heteroskedasticity a la Lewbel (2012)
text: R/ - R functions implementing methodology lewbel2012.tex - LaTeX source file lewbel2012.pdf - Compiled PDF document Supporting files (.aux, .log, .synctex.gz) tests/ - Unit tests vignettes/ - Package vignettes

title: A package for identification through heteroskedasticity a la Lewbel (2012)
text: package uses pre-commit hooks ensure code quality consistency.

title: A package for identification through heteroskedasticity a la Lewbel (2012)
text: Install pre-commit (requires Python): Install git hooks: (Optional) Run hooks files: pre-commit hooks automatically: - Format R code using tidyverse style guide (styler) - Check code quality issues (lintr) - Check spelling - Ensure R code parsable - Remove trailing whitespace fix file endings - Prevent committing large files common R artifacts
code: pip install pre-commit pre-commit install pre-commit run --all-files

title: A package for identification through heteroskedasticity a la Lewbel (2012)
text: project explores various approaches identifying testing heteroskedasticity econometric models, particular attention modern identification strategies.

title: A package for identification through heteroskedasticity a la Lewbel (2012)
text: Lewbel, . (2012). Using heteroscedasticity identify estimate mismeasured endogenous regressor models. Journal Business & Economic Statistics, 30(1), 67-80.
